transcript:
HCP: hi, what can i do for you today?
Patient: hello doctor, so um, i've been feeling really tired for a couple of weeks now
HCP: tired? any specific symptoms?
Patient: yeah, just exhausted like, and i have this really bad headache every morning
HCP: where exactly is the headache?
Patient: behind my eyes mostly, and it gets worse if i look down or bend over
HCP: any eye pain?
Patient: yeah, kind of aching when i try to read
HCP: let me check your eyes, hold still please
HCP: pupils look equal and reactive, good vision
HCP: now, how about your back? any pain?
Patient: oh yeah, my lower back is killing me, especially after sitting at work
HCP: is it stiff?
Patient: very stiff, can't really bend down to tie my shoes
HCP: any history of back problems?
Patient: not really, just started since i started working from home more
HCP: any meds you're taking?
Patient: nothing prescribed, just taking ibuprofen over the counter
HCP: any allergies?
Patient: no, never had any reactions
HCP: ok, let me do a quick exam of your neck
HCP: tenderness here, and your muscles feel tight
HCP: so you've got fatigue and headaches, plus back pain
Patient: that's right, it's been going on for ages
HCP: sounds like tension-type headaches and maybe some cervical strain
HCP: i'll give you some advice, first off, stop taking ibuprofen unless necessary
HCP: try some heat packs for the back
HCP: also, try to stretch a bit more during the day
Patient: sounds reasonable
HCP: and if the headache doesn't improve in a week, come back and we might need some further tests
Patient: thanks doctor, appreciate the help

chief_complaint.evidence_span: feeling really tired for a couple of weeks now

hpi.text: tired for a couple of weeks, headache behind eyes worsened by looking down, eye ache when reading, lower back pain after sitting, stiff back can't bend, working from home more, taking ibuprofen over the counter, no allergies, fatigue and headaches and back pain going on for ages
hpi.evidence_span: feeling really tired for a couple of weeks now

meds.evidence_span: just taking ibuprofen over the counter

exam.text: pupils equal and reactive, good vision, neck tenderness, muscles tight
exam.evidence_span: pupils look equal and reactive, good vision

items.evidence_span: stop taking ibuprofen unless necessary

items.action: try some heat packs for the back
items.evidence_span: try some heat packs for the back

items.action: stretch a bit more during the day
items.evidence_span: try to stretch a bit more during the day

transcript:
HCP: hi there, name?
Patient: sarah, sarah jones
HCP: ok sarah, what brings you in today?
Patient: so i've got this really bad ankle sprain from yesterday
HCP: right, how did it happen?
Patient: i was walking the dog and tripped over a branch, landed on my left foot
HCP: and it was painful immediately?
Patient: yeah, couldn't put any weight on it, was crying actually
HCP: ok, any swelling or bruising?
Patient: yeah, it's swollen up quite a bit and there's a big bruise on the outside
HCP: let me take a look, any previous ankle injuries?
Patient: no, never had one before
HCP: pmh?
Patient: no chronic conditions
HCP: meds or allergies?
Patient: no allergies, take multivitamins
HCP: ok, i'll examine it now, foot looks swollen, tenderness on the lateral side
HCP: can you walk on it?
Patient: no, not really, just a few steps
HCP: okay, diagnosis is a grade two lateral ankle sprain
HCP: plan is rest, ice, compression, elevate, crutches if needed, and pain relief with ibuprofen
HCP: come back in a week if not improving
HCP: any other questions?

hpi.text: bad ankle sprain from yesterday, tripped over a branch, landed on left foot, painful immediately, swollen, bruised
hpi.evidence_span: so i've got this really bad ankle sprain from yesterday

exam.text: foot looks swollen, tenderness on the lateral side
exam.evidence_span: foot looks swollen, tenderness on the lateral side

items.evidence_span: rest, ice, compression, elevate, crutches
pain relief with ibuprofen

items.action: return in a week if not improving
items.evidence_span: come back in a week if not improving

transcript:
HCP: hi there, how are you feeling today
Patient: not great, really tired and just generally unwell
HCP: any specific symptoms
Patient: yeah, my knee hurts a lot when I walk and I have a cough that won't stop
HCP: how long has the cough been going on
Patient: started about a week ago, started with a bit of a chesty cough
HCP: any shortness of breath or wheezing
Patient: no, just a tickle in the chest
HCP: ok, let me check your knee, any swelling or redness
Patient: no swelling, just feels stiff in the morning
HCP: range of motion okay?
Patient: yeah, just a bit of pain if I bend it too far
HCP: knee feels okay, let's look at the chest, lungs sound clear
HCP: so sounds like a viral cough and maybe some stiffness in that knee
Patient: yeah, that sums it up
HCP: anything else you're taking for it
Patient: just some ibuprofen for the pain
HCP: allergies
Patient: no known allergies
HCP: any past medical history
Patient: just had a cold last month
HCP: no other conditions I can think of
HCP: for the cough, try drinking plenty of fluids and maybe some honey if you like
HCP: for the knee, keep moving gently and ibuprofen as needed
HCP: if the cough gets worse or you feel breathless, come back
HCP: otherwise, feel better soon

chief_complaint.evidence_span: really tired and just generally unwell

hpi.text: knee hurts when walking, cough lasting a week, chesty cough, morning stiffness, no swelling, no shortness of breath

pmh.evidence_span: just had a cold last month

meds.evidence_span: just some ibuprofen for the pain

exam.text: knee no swelling or redness, range of motion okay with pain on bending, lungs clear

transcript:
HCP: hello, can I get a name?
Patient: yeah sure, it's sarah jenkins
HCP: ok sarah, what brings you in today?
Patient: well um i've got this cough that's been going on for about a week now and my chest feels tight
HCP: right, so it's a cough and chest tightness. Does it bother you at night?
Patient: yeah it wakes me up sometimes, especially when i lie flat
HCP: do you have any shortness of breath between your chest tightness?
Patient: not really, just like the tightness but i can't seem to catch my breath easily when i run up stairs
HCP: and have you had any chest pain or palpitations?
Patient: no chest pain, just the tightness feeling
HCP: ok, let's check your background. Any past medical conditions?
Patient: well i have asthma diagnosed back in 2015
HCP: and what medications are you currently on?
Patient: i take ventolin when i feel like it, and fluticasone twice a day
HCP: any allergies to medications or food?
Patient: no i don't think so, maybe peanuts but that's it
HCP: ok, let me listen to your lungs
Patient: okay
HCP: hmm, wheezing heard in both lower zones, lungs are hyper-resonant to percussion
HCP: looks like an acute exacerbation of your asthma, we'll need to step up your treatment
Patient: oh no, that sounds serious
HCP: not too bad if we manage it early, let's get you some nebulised salbutamol now
HCP: and I'll prescribe a course of prednisone tablets
Patient: okay, how many?
HCP: five tablets a day for three days, take it with food
Patient: thanks doctor, will I need to go back soon?
HCP: come back if the symptoms don't improve in a few days or if you get very breathless

chief_complaint.evidence_span: i've got this cough that's been going on for about a week now and my chest feels tight

hpi.text: cough and chest tightness for 1 week, wakes up at night, worse on exertion
hpi.evidence_span: it wakes me up sometimes, especially when i lie flat

pmh.evidence_span: i have asthma diagnosed back in 2015

meds.evidence_span: i take ventolin when i feel like it

exam.text: wheezing in both lower zones, lungs hyper-resonant
exam.evidence_span: wheezing heard in both lower zones, lungs are hyper-resonant to percussion

items.evidence_span: nebulised salbutamol
prescribe a course of prednisone tablets

items.action: five tablets daily for 3 days with food
items.evidence_span: five tablets a day for three days, take it with food

items.action: return if no improvement or very breathless
items.evidence_span: come back if the symptoms don't improve in a few days or if you get very breathless

transcript:
HCP: Hi, good morning, how are you feeling today
Patient: um, well, not great, honestly
HCP: What seems to be the trouble then
Patient: so, i've got this really bad knee, like, it just won't let me walk properly
HCP: Okay, how long have you had this knee pain
Patient: probably since like, last monday, i think
HCP: And anything else bothering you
Patient: yeah, i've been coughing a lot too, and i feel quite tired
HCP: Uh-huh, and any specific conditions or medications you're on
Patient: yeah, i have high blood pressure, and i take lisinopril, and also some statins for my cholesterol
HCP: And any allergies
Patient: no, i don't think so, maybe penicillin when i was younger but don't take it now
HCP: Okay, let me have a look at that knee
Patient: yeah sure
HCP: Um, i see some swelling here, and the joint feels warm to the touch
Patient: wow, it does feel really hot
HCP: And the knee is quite tender when you press on it
Patient: yes, it hurts to touch
HCP: Let's check your blood pressure, please roll up your sleeve
Patient: sure
HCP: 140 over 90, a bit elevated
Patient: oh no, is that bad
HCP: Not too bad, but let's keep an eye on it, and let's check your lungs too
Patient: sure
HCP: Breathing sounds clear, no wheeze heard
Patient: phew, good
HCP: And about that cough, how many times a day
Patient: maybe 20 times, especially at night
HCP: And any mucus
Patient: yeah, it's clear and watery, nothing yellow or green
HCP: Okay, so we have three issues: the knee, the cough, and the blood pressure
Patient: yes, that sounds about right
HCP: For the knee, it looks like a flare-up of arthritis or maybe a sprain
Patient: oh, i thought it was just the weather
HCP: The weather doesn't usually cause swelling and warmth, so let's get you a referral to physio for the knee
Patient: okay, thanks
HCP: For the cough, it sounds like a viral upper respiratory infection
Patient: oh, like a cold
HCP: Yes, just rest and fluids, and maybe some salt water gargles
Patient: okay, i'll try that
HCP: And for the blood pressure, continue with your lisinopril and statins
Patient: right
HCP: Don't take any new meds unless prescribed
Patient: ok
HCP: If the cough gets worse or you get a fever, come back
Patient: got it
HCP: And if the knee pain is unbearable before physio, take some ibuprofen if you can stomach it
Patient: okay, i'll try
HCP: Alright, anything else
Patient: no, i think that covers it
HCP: Okay, take care

chief_complaint.text: knee pain, cough, high blood pressure
chief_complaint.evidence_span: so, i've got this really bad knee, like, it just won't let me walk properly

hpi.text: knee pain since last monday, coughing a lot, feeling tired, high blood pressure
hpi.evidence_span: probably since like, last monday, i think

exam.text: knee swollen and warm, tender to touch, BP 140 over 90, lungs clear
exam.evidence_span: swelling here, and the joint feels warm to the touch

problems.name: knee inflammation or sprain
problems.evidence_span: flare-up of arthritis or maybe a sprain

items.evidence_span: referral to physio for the knee
rest and fluids
salt water gargles
continue with your lisinopril and statins

items.action: return if cough worsens or fever occurs
items.evidence_span: come back
take some ibuprofen if you can stomach it

transcript:
HCP: hello there, take a seat, what seems to be the problem today?
Patient: hi doctor, um i've got this really bad knee, been throbbing for a couple of weeks now
HCP: which knee is it?
Patient: the left one, mostly hurts when i walk up stairs
HCP: does it swell up?
Patient: yeah, quite swollen actually, and feels hot to touch
HCP: any recent injury?
Patient: no, nothing like that, just been gardening a lot
HCP: okay, any history of arthritis?
Patient: not really, just some back pain from years ago
HCP: any meds you're on?
Patient: just ibuprofen sometimes
HCP: allergies?
Patient: no allergies that i know of
HCP: thanks, let me have a look at your knee
Patient: uh ok
HCP: palpable effusion yes, range of motion limited, tenderness over the joint line
HCP: sounds like a bit of inflammation from overuse, maybe early osteoarthritis flare
HCP: i'll give you some stronger anti-inflammatory, ice packs, and physio referral, avoid stairs for now
HCP: sounds about right, any questions?

pmh.evidence_span: just some back pain from years ago

transcript:
HCP: hello, what can i do for you?
Patient: hi doc, i've been feeling really tired for about a week now
HCP: is that the main issue?
Patient: yeah, just feeling lethargic all the time, and my knees hurt a bit when i walk
HCP: any swelling or redness?
Patient: no, nothing visible, just stiff and sore
HCP: any history of arthritis?
Patient: no, never had any issues before
HCP: any meds?
Patient: i take multivitamins in the morning
HCP: any allergies?
Patient: no, never been allergic to anything
HCP: ok, let me check your knees, please lift your trousers
Patient: uh sure, thanks
HCP: range of motion is okay but there is some tenderness on palpation of the patella
HCP: no effusion or redness noted
HCP: sounds like overuse or early onset osteoarthritis, i'll refer you to physio for some exercises
HCP: keep moving but avoid high impact for now
HCP: if the pain gets worse, come back to see me

transcript:
HCP: good morning, how can I help you today
Patient: hi doc, i've been feeling absolutely rubbish for about a week now
HCP: right, what seems to be the problem
Patient: well, i've got this really bad cough, like a hacking cough that won't stop, and my chest feels tight
HCP: is there any phlegm
Patient: yeah, lots of it, it's green and thick, and i keep waking up at night coughing
HCP: okay, do you have any wheezing or shortness of breath
Patient: sometimes i feel like i can't get a full breath when i'm lying down, but it's mostly just the cough
HCP: have you had any fevers
Patient: not really, just feel generally run down
HCP: let's check your history, any long-term conditions
Patient: i have asthma, i've been using my ventolin quite a bit lately
HCP: and any other meds
Patient: i take salbutamol twice a day, and some panto
HCP: allergies?
Patient: no, nothing like that
HCP: right, let me listen to your chest now, hold on, can you take your shirt off
Patient: sure
HCP: okay, deep breath in
Patient: okay
HCP: deep breath out
Patient: okay
HCP: and turn a little to the left
Patient: okay
HCP: and to the right
Patient: okay
HCP: now listen with the bell first, okay
Patient: okay
HCP: okay, now the diaphragm
Patient: okay
HCP: i'm hearing some wheezing in the lower zones, and it sounds congested
HCP: any pain in your chest when you cough
Patient: a little bit, just a sharp twinge sometimes
HCP: let's check your blood pressure and temperature
Patient: sure
HCP: okay, BP is 140 over 90, temp is 37.2
HCP: so you've got asthma exacerbated by a likely bronchitis
Patient: oh no, that's not good
HCP: yeah, sounds like an infection has triggered your asthma
Patient: i've been feeling so tired all day
HCP: let's get you on a steroid course to calm that down
Patient: okay
HCP: i'll write you a prescription for prednisolone
Patient: thanks
HCP: also, you need to step up your inhaler use
Patient: okay
HCP: use your ventolin every four hours as needed
Patient: right
HCP: and continue your regular salbutamol
Patient: got it
HCP: and for the cough, take a couple of ibuprofen if the pain is bad
Patient: okay
HCP: and try to stay well hydrated
Patient: sure
HCP: come back in three days for a review
Patient: okay, thanks doc
HCP: don't hesitate to ring if you get worse before then
Patient: thanks

chief_complaint.evidence_span: i've got this really bad cough

hpi.text: feeling rubbish for a week, hacking cough, green thick phlegm, waking up at night, feeling like can't get full breath, no fever, generally run down, sharp twinge in chest sometimes
hpi.evidence_span: i've been feeling absolutely rubbish for about a week now

meds.evidence_span: i've been using my ventolin quite a bit lately
i take salbutamol twice a day

transcript:
HCP: hi there, how are you feeling today?
Patient: um, pretty rough actually, been struggling with this cough for a while
HCP: how long have you had the cough?
Patient: like, almost two weeks now, started with a bit of a runny nose
HCP: any wheezing or shortness of breath?
Patient: yeah, kinda feels tight in my chest sometimes, especially at night
HCP: have you been taking any inhalers?
Patient: yeah, got ventolin prescribed last month, use it a few times a day
HCP: any other conditions?
Patient: uh, high blood pressure, on some meds
HCP: what meds exactly?
Patient: lisinopril and amlodipine, plus the ventolin
HCP: allergies?
Patient: not really, maybe dust sometimes
HCP: okay, let me listen to your lungs
HCP: okay, let me feel your chest too
HCP: sounds a bit wheezy, chest feels tight
HCP: so it seems like your asthma is flaring up again
HCP: I'll review your inhaler technique and maybe adjust your puffer
HCP: keep using ventolin as needed, but if it's not settling in 3 days, give me a call

hpi.text: cough for almost two weeks, started with runny nose, tight chest at night
